Star Symbols are for On the Cuss of things 'NM'=New Moon 'O'=Full Moon 'LQ'=Last Quarter 'FQ'=First Quarter A Few of Todays and Tommorows Famous Birthdays and Then The Rest
Start Day Day This Time Days To Go Start Date Years From Start Date Same Dayer Count Age For Next Same Dayer Born On A Leap Year Days After A New Moon Next/Last B-Day On A Full Moon Description
Wed Fri 0 20-02-0509 1500 218 1505 No FQ 22 N/A -- Exact 1500 Years Ago --
Mon Fri 0 20-02-1009 1000 145 1004 No O 25 N/A -- Exact 1000 Years Ago --
Sat Fri 0 20-02-1509 500 72 502 No O 0 N/A -- Exact 500 Years Ago --
Mon Fri 0 20-02-1673 336 49 340 No NM 3 N/A The first recorded wine auction took place in London.
Wed Fri 0 20-02-1709 300 44 305 No FQ 10 N/A -- Exact 300 Years Ago --
Tue Fri 0 20-02-1725 284 41 294 No FQ 6 N/A The first known Indian scalping by white men was reported in the New Hampshire colony.
Mon Fri 0 20-02-1792 217 32 221 Yes LQ 27 N/A U.S. President George Washington signed the Postal Service Act thereby creating the U.S. Post Office.
Mon Fri 0 20-02-1809 200 29 204 No NM 5 N/A -- Exact 200 Years Ago --
Mon Fri 0 20-02-1809 200 29 204 No NM 5 N/A The U.S. Supreme Court ruled the power of the federal government was greater than that of any individual state.
Mon Fri 0 20-02-1815 194 28 198 No FQ 10 N/A The USS Constitution, under Captain Charles Stewart fought the British ships Cyane and Levant. The Constitution captures both, but lost the Levant after encountering a British squadron. The Constitution and the Cyane returned to New York safely on May 15, 1815. The Cyane was purchased and became the USS Cyane.
Wed Fri 0 20-02-1839 170 25 175 No NM 5 N/A The U.S. Congress prohibited dueling in the District of Columbia.
Tue Fri 0 20-02-1872 137 21 147 Yes FQ 10 N/A Luther Crowell received a patent for a machine that manufactured paper bags.
Tue Fri 0 20-02-1872 137 21 147 Yes FQ 10 N/A Silas Noble and J.P. Cooley patented the toothpick manufacturing machine.
Tue Fri 0 20-02-1872 137 21 147 Yes FQ 10 N/A The Metropolitan Museum of Art opened in New York City.
Thu Fri 0 20-02-1873 136 20 142 No LQ 22 N/A The University of California got its first Medical School.
Fri Fri 0 20-02-1880 129 18* 136 Yes FQ 9 N/A The American Bell Company was incorporated.
Wed Fri 0 20-02-1901 108 16 113 No NM 0 1989 2027 The first territorial legislature of Hawaii convened.
Thu Fri 0 20-02-1902 107 16 113 No FQ 11 1989 2027 B Day:-Ansel Adams, knows how to handle a camera.
Sat Fri 0 20-02-1909 100 14 102 No NM * 0 1989 2027 -- Exact 100 Years Ago --
Sun Fri 0 20-02-1921 88 13 91 No FQ 11 1989 2027 The motion picture "The Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse" was released starring Rudolph Valentino.
Sun Fri 0 20-02-1927 82 12 85 No O 17 1989 2027 B DAY:-Sidney Poitier - Actor
Fri Fri 0 20-02-1931 78 11* 85 No NM 2 1989 2027 The U.S. Congress allowed California to build the Oakland Bay Bridge.
Mon Fri 0 20-02-1933 76 11 80 No LQ 25 1989 2027 The U.S. House of Representatives completed congressional action on the amendment to repeal Prohibition.
Sun Fri 0 20-02-1944 65 10 68 Yes LQ 25 1989 2027 "Big Week" began as U.S. bombers began raiding German aircraft manufacturing centers during World War II.
Wed Fri 0 20-02-1946 63 10 68 No O 17 1989 2027 B DAY:-Sandy Duncan - Actress
Sun Fri 0 20-02-1949 60 9 63 No LQ* 21 1989 2027 B DAY:-Ivana Trump - Celebrity
Sun Fri 0 20-02-1949 60 9 63 No LQ* 21 1989 2027 B DAY:-Jennifer O'Neill - Actress
Wed Fri 0 20-02-1952 57 9 62 Yes LQ 24 1989 2027 "The African Queen" opened at the Capitol Theatre in New York City.
Wed Fri 0 20-02-1952 57 9 62 Yes LQ 24 1989 2027 Emmett L. Ashford became the first black umpire in organized baseball. He was authorized to be a substitute in the Southwestern International League.
Sat Fri 0 20-02-1954 55 8 57 No O 16 1989 2027 B DAY:-Patty Hearst - Celebrity
Thu Fri 0 20-02-1958 51 8 57 No NM 1 1989 2027 Racing jockey Eddie Arcaro got win number 4,000, as he rode the winner at Santa Anita race track in Southern California.
Fri Fri 0 20-02-1959 50 7* 57 No FQ 12 1989 2027 -- Exact Fifty Years Ago --
Tue Fri 0 20-02-1962 47 8 57 No O 14 1989 2027 John Glenn made space history when he orbited the world three times in 4 hours, 55 minutes. He was the first American to orbit the Earth. He was aboard the Friendship 7 Mercury capsule.
Tue Fri 0 20-02-1962 47 8 57 No O 14 1989 2027 Space -- US spaceman orbits Earth
Sat Fri 0 20-02-1965 44 6 46 No O 18 1989 2027 Ranger 8 crashed on the moon after sending back thousands of pictures of its surface.
Thu Fri 0 20-02-1986 23 4 29 No FQ 10 1989 2027 Space -- Soviets launch space station Mir
Fri Fri 0 20-02-1987 22 3* 29 No O 21 1989 2027 A bomb exploded in a computer store in Salt Lake City, UT. The blast was blamed on the Unabomber.
Fri Fri 0 20-02-1987 22 3* 29 No O 21 1989 2027 After 11 years, David Hartman left ABC’s "Good Morning America."
Mon Fri 0 20-02-1989 20 3 24 No O * 14 1989 2027 -- Exact Twenty Years Ago --
Sat Fri 0 20-02-1993 16 2 18 No LQ 28 1989 2027 Police -- Two boys charged with toddler's murder
Sat Fri 0 20-02-1993 16 2 18 No LQ 28 1989 2027 Two ten-year-old boys were charged by police in Liverpool, England, in the abduction and death of a toddler. The two boys were later convicted.
Fri Fri 0 20-02-1998 11 2* 18 No LQ 22 1989 2027 American Tara Lipinski, at age 15, became the youngest gold medalist in winter Olympics history when she won the ladies' figure skating title at Nagano, Japan.
Sat Fri 0 20-02-1999 10 1 12 No NM 3 1989 2027 -- Exact Ten Years Ago --
Tue Fri 0 20-02-2001 8 2 18 No LQ 26 1989 2027 FBI Agent Robert Phillip Hanssen was arrested and charged with spying for the Russians for 15 years.
Wed Fri 0 20-02-2002 7 2 12 No FQ* 8 1989 2027 In Reqa Al-Gharbiya, Egypt, a fire raced through a train killing at least 370 people and injuring at least 65.
Fri Fri 0 20-02-2009 0 0* 7 No LQ 24 1989 2027 Spring half term End
